{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_74753fdc3e31422fa92e36dc","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Scope-Based Key Tracking Design for ARMOR YAML Parser\n2\t\n3\t## Executive Summary\n4\t\n5\tThis document outlines the design for a scope-based key tracking system that properly handles YAML mapping keys within their correct scope context. The current implementation has limitations in handling nested structures and scope transitions. This design provides a hierarchical scope system with proper key isolation and comprehensive duplicate detection.\n6\t\n7\t## Current Implementation Analysis\n8\t\n9\t### Existing Structure (from `syntax_detector.rs`)\n10\t\n11\t```rust\n12\tstruct StructureState {\n13\t    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,  // Tracks structure types only\n14\t    current_keys: HashSet<String>,          // Single-level key tracking\n15\t    prev_indent: usize,                     // Previous indentation level\n16\t}\n17\t```\n18\t\n19\t### Current Limitations\n20\t\n21\t1. **No Scope Hierarchy**: Only tracks keys at one level at a time\n22\t2. **Aggressive Key Clearing**: Clears all keys when indentation decreases\n23\t3. **No Scope Preservation**: Loses parent scope keys when entering nested structures\n24\t4. **Context Type Only**: `context_stack` only tracks structure types, not keys\n25\t5. **False Positives**: May report duplicates when keys appear in different scopes\n26\t\n27\t### Current Behavior\n28\t\n29\t```yaml\n30\t# Current implementation behavior:\n31\tservices:\n32\t  web:\n33\t    host: localhost      # Key \"host\" added to current_keys\n34\t    port: 8080           # Key \"port\" added to current_keys\n35\t  database:\n36\t    host: db.example.com  # ERROR: \"host\" reported as duplicate!\n37\t    port: 5432            # ERROR: \"port\" reported as duplicate!\n38\t```\n39\t\n40\tThe current implementation incorrectly reports duplicates because it doesn't distinguish between different mapping scopes (`services.web` vs `services.database`).\n41\t\n42\t## Proposed Design\n43\t\n44\t### Core Concept: Hierarchical Scope Stack\n45\t\n46\tA **scope** represents a mapping context where keys are defined and must be unique. Each scope is associated with a specific indentation level and maintains its own set of keys.\n47\t\n48\t#### Scope Definition\n49\t\n50\t```rust\n51\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n52\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n53\tstruct Scope {\n54\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n55\t    indent_level: usize,\n56\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n57\t    keys: HashSet<String>,\n58\t    /// Line number where this scope started (for error reporting)\n59\t    start_line: usize,\n60\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n61\t    parent_key: Option<String>,\n62\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n63\t    is_flow_style: bool,\n64\t}\n65\t```\n66\t\n67\t#### Scope Stack\n68\t\n69\t```rust\n70\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n71\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n72\tstruct ScopeStack {\n73\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n74\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n75\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n76\t    base_indent: usize,\n77\t}\n78\t\n79\timpl ScopeStack {\n80\t    /// Get the scope for a specific indentation level\n81\t    fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope>;\n82\t    \n83\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n84\t    fn current_scope(&self) -> Option<&Scope>;\n85\t    \n86\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n87\t    fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>);\n88\t    \n89\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n90\t    fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize);\n91\t    \n92\t    /// Check if a key already exists in current scope\n93\t    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool;\n94\t    \n95\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n96\t    fn add_key(&mut self, key: String, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError>;\n97\t}\n98\t```\n99\t\n100\t### Scope Transition Algorithm\n101\t\n102\t#### 1. Entering a Scope (Indentation Increase)\n103\t\n104\t```yaml\n105\tservices:\n106\t  web:              # ← ENTER scope for \"services.web\"\n107\t    host: localhost\n108\t    port: 8080\n109\t```\n110\t\n111\t**Algorithm:**\n112\t```rust\n113\tfn on_indent_increase(indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n114\t    // Calculate scope level from indentation\n115\t    let scope_level = indent_level / base_indent;\n116\t    \n117\t    // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n118\t    if let Some(existing_scope) = get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n119\t        // Re-enter existing scope (e.g., sibling mapping)\n120\t        // Don't clear keys - they persist within the scope\n121\t        set_current_scope(existing_scope);\n122\t    } else {\n123\t        // Create new scope for this indentation level\n124\t        let new_scope = Scope {\n125\t            indent_level,\n126\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n127\t            start_line: line,\n128\t            parent_key,\n129\t            is_flow_style: false,\n130\t        };\n131\t        push_scope(new_scope);\n132\t    }\n133\t}\n134\t```\n135\t\n136\t#### 2. Exiting a Scope (Indentation Decrease)\n137\t\n138\t```yaml\n139\tservices:\n140\t  web:\n141\t    host: localhost\n142\t    port: 8080\n143\tdatabase:           # ← EXIT scope for \"services.web\"\n144\t  host: db.example.com\n145\t```\n146\t\n147\t**Algorithm:**\n148\t```rust\n149\tfn on_indent_decrease(new_indent_level: usize) {\n150\t    // Pop scopes until we find the target scope\n151\t    while current_scope().indent_level > new_indent_level {\n152\t        pop_scope();\n153\t    }\n154\t    \n155\t    // Set current to the scope matching new_indent_level\n156\t    set_current_scope(get_scope_at_level(new_indent_level));\n157\t}\n158\t```\n159\t\n160\t**Key Preservation**: When exiting a scope, we **preserve** the scope and its keys. If we re-enter that scope later (e.g., a sibling mapping), the keys are still available for duplicate checking.\n161\t\n162\t### Key Detection and Classification\n163\t\n164\t#### Key Context Types\n165\t\n166\t```rust\n167\t/// Classification of how a key is used in the YAML document\n168\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n169\tenum KeyContext {\n170\t    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n171\t    InlineScalar {\n172\t        key: String,\n173\t        value: String,\n174\t    },\n175\t    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n176\t    ParentMapping {\n177\t        key: String,\n178\t    },\n179\t    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n180\t    ParentSequence {\n181\t        key: String,\n182\t    },\n183\t    /// Key in flow mapping: \"{key: value, key2: value2}\"\n184\t    FlowMapping {\n185\t        key: String,\n186\t    },\n187\t    /// Key with flow sequence value: \"key: [val1, val2]\"\n188\t    FlowSequenceValue {\n189\t        key: String,\n190\t    },\n191\t    /// Key in explicit mapping: \"? key\\n: value\"\n192\t    ExplicitMapping {\n193\t        key: String,\n194\t    },\n195\t}\n196\t```\n197\t\n198\t#### Key Extraction Algorithm\n199\t\n200\t```rust\n201\tfn extract_key_context(line: &str, line_num: usize) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n202\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n203\t    \n204\t    // Skip if not a key-value line\n205\t    if !trimmed.contains(':') {\n206\t        return None;\n207\t    }\n208\t    \n209\t    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':').unwrap();\n210\t    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n211\t    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos+1..];\n212\t    \n213\t    // Skip if key is empty or contains special characters\n214\t    let key = key_part.trim();\n215\t    if key.is_empty() || key.contains(['?', '&', '*', '!', '[', ']', '{', '}']) {\n216\t        return None;\n217\t    }\n218\t    \n219\t    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n220\t    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n221\t        // \"key:\\n  ...\" → Parent key with nested content\n222\t        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n223\t    } else if after_colon.contains('[') {\n224\t        // \"key: [val1, val2]\" → Key with flow sequence\n225\t        KeyContext::FlowSequenceValue { key: key.to_string() }\n226\t    } else if after_colon.contains('{') {\n227\t        // \"key: {nested: value}\" → Key with flow mapping\n228\t        KeyContext::FlowMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n229\t    } else {\n230\t        // \"key: value\" → Inline scalar value\n231\t        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n232\t            key: key.to_string(),\n233\t            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n234\t        }\n235\t    };\n236\t    \n237\t    Some(context)\n238\t}\n239\t```\n240\t\n241\t### Duplicate Detection Algorithm\n242\t\n243\t```rust\n244\tfn detect_duplicate_keys(\n245\t    scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack,\n246\t    line: &str,\n247\t    line_num: usize,\n248\t) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n249\t    let mut errors = Vec::new();\n250\t    \n251\t    // Skip flow-style contexts (handled separately)\n252\t    if is_in_flow_context(line) {\n253\t        return errors;\n254\t    }\n255\t    \n256\t    let indent = get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n257\t    \n258\t    // Handle scope transitions\n259\t    match indent.cmp(&current_indent_level()) {\n260\t        Ordering::Greater => {\n261\t            // Entering deeper scope\n262\t            if let Some(KeyContext::ParentMapping { key }) = extract_key_context(line, line_num) {\n263\t                scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num, Some(key));\n264\t            }\n265\t            return errors; // Parent keys don't create duplicate checks\n266\t        }\n267\t        Ordering::Less => {\n268\t            // Exiting to parent scope\n269\t            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n270\t        }\n271\t        Ordering::Equal => {\n272\t            // Same scope - continue checking for duplicates\n273\t        }\n274\t    }\n275\t    \n276\t    // Extract and check key\n277\t    if let Some(key_context) = extract_key_context(line, line_num) {\n278\t        match key_context {\n279\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } => {\n280\t                // Parent keys create new scope, handled above\n281\t            }\n282\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } |\n283\t            KeyContext::FlowMapping { key, .. } |\n284\t            KeyContext::FlowSequenceValue { key, .. } => {\n285\t                // Check for duplicate in current scope\n286\t                if scope_stack.contains_key(&key) {\n287\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n288\t                        format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n289\t                        format!(\"duplicate key '{}' in mapping scope\", key)\n290\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_code(ErrorCode::KEY_DUPLICATE));\n291\t                } else {\n292\t                    scope_stack.add_key(key, line_num);\n293\t                }\n294\t            }\n295\t            _ => {}\n296\t        }\n297\t    }\n298\t    \n299\t    errors\n300\t}\n301\t```\n302\t\n303\t## Handling Complex Scenarios\n304\t\n305\t### Scenario 1: Deeply Nested Mappings\n306\t\n307\t```yaml\n308\tlevel1:\n309\t  level2:\n310\t    level3:\n311\t      key1: value1\n312\t      key2: value2\n313\t    key3: value3\n314\t  key4: value4\n315\tkey5: value5\n316\t```\n317\t\n318\t**Scope Stack Evolution:**\n319\t\n320\t1. Line 1 (`level1:`): Enter scope level 0 with parent_key=\"level1\"\n321\t2. Line 2 (`  level2:`): Enter scope level 2 with parent_key=\"level2\"\n322\t3. Line 3 (`    level3:`): Enter scope level 4 with parent_key=\"level3\"\n323\t4. Line 4 (`      key1:`): Add \"key1\" to scope at level 4\n324\t5. Line 5 (`      key2:`): Add \"key2\" to scope at level 4\n325\t6. Line 6 (`    key3:`): Exit to scope level 2, add \"key3\"\n326\t7. Line 7 (`  key4:`): Exit to scope level 0, add \"key4\"\n327\t8. Line 8 (`key5:`): Exit to root scope, add \"key5\"\n328\t\n329\t**Result**: Each \"key\" is tracked in its appropriate scope, no false duplicates.\n330\t\n331\t### Scenario 2: Sibling Mappings with Same Keys\n332\t\n333\t```yaml\n334\tservices:\n335\t  web:\n336\t    host: localhost\n337\t    port: 8080\n338\t  database:\n339\t    host: db.example.com\n340\t    port: 5432\n341\t```\n342\t\n343\t**Scope Stack Evolution:**\n344\t\n345\t1. Line 1 (`services:`): Root scope (level 0)\n346\t2. Line 2 (`  web:`): Enter scope level 2, parent_key=\"web\"\n347\t3. Line 3 (`    host:`): Add \"host\" to scope level 4\n348\t4. Line 4 (`    port:`): Add \"port\" to scope level 4\n349\t5. Line 5 (`  database:`): Exit to scope level 2, **preserve scope level 4 keys**\n350\t6. Line 6 (`    host:`): **Enter existing scope level 4**, check \"host\" against **fresh keys**\n351\t7. Line 7 (`    port:`): Add \"port\" to scope level 4\n352\t\n353\t**Critical Decision**: When exiting scope level 4 (going to level 2), do we:\n354\t- **Option A**: Clear the scope level 4 keys (current behavior)\n355\t- **Option B**: Preserve the scope level 4 keys for potential re-entry\n356\t\n357\t**Correct Behavior is Option A**: Each sibling mapping is a **new scope**. The \"host\" in `services.web` and the \"host\" in `services.database` are in **different scopes**, so they should both be allowed.\n358\t\n359\t**Revised Algorithm**:\n360\t```rust\n361\tfn on_indent_decrease(new_indent_level: usize) {\n362\t    // Clear all scopes deeper than new_indent_level\n363\t    scopes.retain(|scope| scope.indent_level <= new_indent_level);\n364\t    set_current_scope(new_indent_level);\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\tfn on_indent_increase(indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n368\t    // Always create a FRESH scope when entering a deeper level\n369\t    let new_scope = Scope {\n370\t        indent_level,\n371\t        keys: HashSet::new(),  // Fresh keys\n372\t        start_line: line,\n373\t        parent_key,\n374\t        is_flow_style: false,\n375\t    };\n376\t    push_scope(new_scope);\n377\t}\n378\t```\n379\t\n380\t### Scenario 3: Mixed Scalar and Mapping Values\n381\t\n382\t```yaml\n383\tsimple_key: scalar_value\n384\tmapping_key:\n385\t  nested1: value1\n386\t  nested2: value2\n387\tsequence_key:\n388\t  - item1\n389\t  - item2\n390\tflow_key: {inline: value, another: value2}\n391\t```\n392\t\n393\t**Handling**:\n394\t- `simple_key`: Add \"simple_key\" to root scope\n395\t- `mapping_key`: Add \"mapping_key\" to root scope, **then** enter nested scope\n396\t- `sequence_key`: Add \"sequence_key\" to root scope, enter sequence scope\n397\t- `flow_key`: Add \"flow_key\" to root scope, parse flow mapping separately\n398\t\n399\t### Scenario 4: Flow-Style Mappings\n400\t\n401\t```yaml\n402\tinline: {key1: value1, key2: value2}\n403\tmultiline: {\n404\t  key3: value3,\n405\t  key4: value4\n406\t}\n407\t```\n408\t\n409\t**Handling**:\n410\t- Detect flow context by checking for `{` character\n411\t- Extract keys using specialized flow parser\n412\t- Track duplicates within the flow mapping scope only\n413\t- Don't mix flow-style keys with block-style keys\n414\t\n415\t## Migration Path from Current Implementation\n416\t\n417\t### Phase 1: Add Scope Stack Alongside Current Implementation\n418\t\n419\t**Changes to `StructureState`:**\n420\t\n421\t```rust\n422\tstruct StructureState {\n423\t    // NEW: Hierarchical scope tracking\n424\t    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n425\t    \n426\t    // LEGACY: Keep for backward compatibility during migration\n427\t    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,\n428\t    current_keys: HashSet<String>,\n429\t    prev_indent: usize,\n430\t    \n431\t    // Migration flag\n432\t    use_legacy_tracking: bool,\n433\t}\n434\t```\n435\t\n436\t**Dual Implementation:**\n437\t```rust\n438\tfn detect_duplicate_key_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n439\t    if self.use_legacy_tracking {\n440\t        // Use current implementation\n441\t        self.legacy_detect_duplicates(line, line_num, errors);\n442\t    } else {\n443\t        // Use new scope-based implementation\n444\t        self.scope_based_detect_duplicates(line, line_num, errors);\n445\t    }\n446\t}\n447\t```\n448\t\n449\t### Phase 2: Feature Flag for New Implementation\n450\t\n451\t**Configuration Update:**\n452\t\n453\t```rust\n454\tpub struct DetectorConfig {\n455\t    // ... existing config ...\n456\t    \n457\t    /// Enable scope-based key tracking (Phase 2 feature)\n458\t    pub use_scope_based_key_tracking: bool,\n459\t}\n460\t\n461\timpl Default for DetectorConfig {\n462\t    fn default() -> Self {\n463\t        Self {\n464\t            // ... existing defaults ...\n465\t            use_scope_based_key_tracking: false,  // Disabled by default during migration\n466\t        }\n467\t    }\n468\t}\n469\t```\n470\t\n471\t### Phase 3: Validation and Testing\n472\t\n473\t**Test Coverage:**\n474\t\n475\t```rust\n476\t#[cfg(test)]\n477\tmod scope_based_tests {\n478\t    #[test]\n479\t    fn test_sibling_mappings_same_keys() {\n480\t        let yaml = \"\n481\tservices:\n482\t  web:\n483\t    host: localhost\n484\t  database:\n485\t    host: db.example.com\n486\t\";\n487\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::with_config(DetectorConfig {\n488\t            use_scope_based_key_tracking: true,\n489\t            ..Default::default()\n490\t        });\n491\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n492\t        assert!(errors.is_empty(), \"Should not report duplicate across siblings\");\n493\t    }\n494\t    \n495\t    #[test]\n496\t    fn test_actual_duplicate_in_same_scope() {\n497\t        let yaml = \"\n498\tconfig:\n499\t  host: localhost\n500\t  host: duplicate\n501\t\";\n502\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::with_config(DetectorConfig {\n503\t            use_scope_based_key_tracking: true,\n504\t            ..Default::default()\n505\t        });\n506\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n507\t        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.code == ErrorCode::KEY_DUPLICATE));\n508\t    }\n509\t    \n510\t    #[test]\n511\t    fn test_deeply_nested_mappings() {\n512\t        let yaml = \"\n513\tlevel1:\n514\t  level2:\n515\t    level3:\n516\t      key: value\n517\t  key: value2\n518\tkey: value3\n519\t\";\n520\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::with_config(DetectorConfig {\n521\t            use_scope_based_key_tracking: true,\n522\t            ..Default::default()\n523\t        });\n524\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n525\t        assert!(errors.is_empty(), \"Should handle deep nesting correctly\");\n526\t    }\n527\t}\n528\t```\n529\t\n530\t### Phase 4: Gradual Rollout\n531\t\n532\t1. **Week 1-2**: Feature flag = false (current behavior)\n533\t2. **Week 3-4**: Feature flag = true in test environments only\n534\t3. **Week 5-6**: Monitor error reports, tune scope detection\n535\t4. **Week 7-8**: Feature flag = true in production\n536\t5. **Week 9+**: Remove legacy code, set feature flag to always true\n537\t\n538\t### Phase 5: Remove Legacy Implementation\n539\t\n540\t```rust\n541\t// Final StructureState (post-migration)\n542\tstruct StructureState {\n543\t    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n544\t    // No legacy fields\n545\t}\n546\t```\n547\t\n548\t## Data Structure Summary\n549\t\n550\t### Complete Scope Tracking Structure\n551\t\n552\t```rust\n553\t/// Hierarchical key tracking with proper scope isolation\n554\tpub struct ScopeBasedKeyTracker {\n555\t    /// Stack of active scopes\n556\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n557\t    /// Base indentation size\n558\t    base_indent: usize,\n559\t    /// Current flow-style context (if any)\n560\t    flow_context: Option<FlowContext>,\n561\t}\n562\t\n563\t/// A single scope in the hierarchy\n564\tpub struct Scope {\n565\t    /// Indentation level for this scope\n566\t    pub indent_level: usize,\n567\t    /// Keys defined in this scope\n568\t    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n569\t    /// Line where this scope started\n570\t    pub start_line: usize,\n571\t    /// Parent key name\n572\t    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n573\t    /// Whether this is a flow-style mapping\n574\t    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n575\t    /// Keys seen in flow context (if is_flow_style)\n576\t    pub flow_keys: HashSet<String>,\n577\t}\n578\t\n579\t/// Flow-style context tracking\n580\tpub struct FlowContext {\n581\t    /// Brackets/braces depth\n582\t    depth: usize,\n583\t    /// Keys seen in current flow mapping\n584\t    keys: HashSet<String>,\n585\t    /// Opening line number\n586\t    start_line: usize,\n587\t}\n588\t```\n589\t\n590\t## Algorithm Complexity\n591\t\n592\t### Time Complexity\n593\t\n594\t- **Scope lookup**: O(1) using indent_level as index\n595\t- **Key duplicate check**: O(1) using HashSet\n596\t- **Scope transition**: O(k) where k = number of scopes to pop (typically small)\n597\t- **Overall line processing**: O(1) amortized per line\n598\t\n599\t### Space Complexity\n600\t\n601\t- **Per scope**: O(m) where m = number of keys in that scope\n602\t- **Total space**: O(n * m_avg) where n = number of active scopes\n603\t- **Typical case**: O(depth * keys_per_scope) where depth ≤ 10 for most YAML files\n604\t\n605\t## Error Messages\n606\t\n607\t### Duplicate Key Error\n608\t\n609\t```\n610\tLine 5: duplicate key 'host' in mapping scope 'services.web'\n611\t  First occurrence: Line 3\n612\t  Current occurrence: Line 5\n613\t  Scope path: services.web\n614\t```\n615\t\n616\t### Enhanced Error Context\n617\t\n618\t```rust\n619\tpub struct DuplicateKeyError {\n620\t    pub key: String,\n621\t    pub scope_path: String,        // e.g., \"services.web\"\n622\t    pub first_line: usize,\n623\t    pub duplicate_line: usize,\n624\t    pub code: ErrorCode,\n625\t}\n626\t\n627\timpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n628\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n629\t        write!(\n630\t            f,\n631\t            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\\n  Current location: Line {}\",\n632\t            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line, self.duplicate_line\n633\t        )\n634\t    }\n635\t}\n636\t```\n637\t\n638\t## Configuration Options\n639\t\n640\t### DetectorConfig Extensions\n641\t\n642\t```rust\n643\tpub struct DetectorConfig {\n644\t    // ... existing fields ...\n645\t    \n646\t    /// Enable scope-based key tracking\n647\t    pub use_scope_based_key_tracking: bool,\n648\t    \n649\t    /// Base indentation size (2 or 4)\n650\t    pub base_indent_size: usize,\n651\t    \n652\t    /// How strict duplicate detection should be\n653\t    pub duplicate_key_strictness: DuplicateKeyStrictness,\n654\t}\n655\t\n656\tpub enum DuplicateKeyStrictness {\n657\t    /// Only check within the same immediate scope\n658\t    SameScopeOnly,\n659\t    /// Check across entire scope hierarchy\n660\t    FullHierarchy,\n661\t    /// Allow duplicates if types differ\n662\t    AllowDifferentTypes,\n663\t}\n664\t```\n665\t\n666\t## Conclusion\n667\t\n668\tThis design provides a robust, hierarchical scope-based key tracking system that:\n669\t\n670\t1. **Properly isolates keys by scope**: Each mapping maintains its own key set\n671\t2. **Handles complex nesting**: Deep hierarchies are tracked correctly\n672\t3. **Supports mixed content**: Scalar values, mappings, and sequences coexist\n673\t4. **Provides clear migration path**: Feature flag and phased rollout\n674\t5. **Maintains performance**: O(1) operations per line for typical cases\n675\t6. **Enhances error messages**: Clear scope path in duplicate reports\n676\t\n677\tThe implementation will be rolled out in phases to ensure backward compatibility and thorough testing before making it the default behavior.\n678\t\n679\t## Next Steps\n680\t\n681\t1. Implement `Scope` and `ScopeStack` structs\n682\t2. Add `ScopeBasedKeyTracker` to `StructureState`\n683\t3. Implement scope transition logic\n684\t4. Add comprehensive test coverage\n685\t5. Create migration plan with feature flags\n686\t6. Monitor and validate in test environments\n687\t7. Gradual rollout to production\n688\t8. Remove legacy implementation\n689\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"91fd6c7c-9ba3-4b5f-abe7-4c3a2c14e4c9","uuid":"92421df2-66ca-4001-b285-fa638c3cd7ac","timestamp":"2026-07-13T18:04:12.890Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/docs/scope_based_key_tracking_design.md","content":"# Scope-Based Key Tracking Design for ARMOR YAML Parser\n\n## Executive Summary\n\nThis document outlines the design for a scope-based key tracking system that properly handles YAML mapping keys within their correct scope context. The current implementation has limitations in handling nested structures and scope transitions. This design provides a hierarchical scope system with proper key isolation and comprehensive duplicate detection.\n\n## Current Implementation Analysis\n\n### Existing Structure (from `syntax_detector.rs`)\n\n```rust\nstruct StructureState {\n    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,  // Tracks structure types only\n    current_keys: HashSet<String>,          // Single-level key tracking\n    prev_indent: usize,                     // Previous indentation level\n}\n```\n\n### Current Limitations\n\n1. **No Scope Hierarchy**: Only tracks keys at one level at a time\n2. **Aggressive Key Clearing**: Clears all keys when indentation decreases\n3. **No Scope Preservation**: Loses parent scope keys when entering nested structures\n4. **Context Type Only**: `context_stack` only tracks structure types, not keys\n5. **False Positives**: May report duplicates when keys appear in different scopes\n\n### Current Behavior\n\n```yaml\n# Current implementation behavior:\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost      # Key \"host\" added to current_keys\n    port: 8080           # Key \"port\" added to current_keys\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com  # ERROR: \"host\" reported as duplicate!\n    port: 5432            # ERROR: \"port\" reported as duplicate!\n```\n\nThe current implementation incorrectly reports duplicates because it doesn't distinguish between different mapping scopes (`services.web` vs `services.database`).\n\n## Proposed Design\n\n### Core Concept: Hierarchical Scope Stack\n\nA **scope** represents a mapping context where keys are defined and must be unique. Each scope is associated with a specific indentation level and maintains its own set of keys.\n\n#### Scope Definition\n\n```rust\n/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct Scope {\n    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n    indent_level: usize,\n    /// Keys defined within this scope\n    keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Line number where this scope started (for error reporting)\n    start_line: usize,\n    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n    parent_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n    is_flow_style: bool,\n}\n```\n\n#### Scope Stack\n\n```rust\n/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct ScopeStack {\n    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n    base_indent: usize,\n}\n\nimpl ScopeStack {\n    /// Get the scope for a specific indentation level\n    fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope>;\n    \n    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n    fn current_scope(&self) -> Option<&Scope>;\n    \n    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n    fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>);\n    \n    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n    fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize);\n    \n    /// Check if a key already exists in current scope\n    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool;\n    \n    /// Add a key to current scope\n    fn add_key(&mut self, key: String, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError>;\n}\n```\n\n### Scope Transition Algorithm\n\n#### 1. Entering a Scope (Indentation Increase)\n\n```yaml\nservices:\n  web:              # ← ENTER scope for \"services.web\"\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n```\n\n**Algorithm:**\n```rust\nfn on_indent_increase(indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n    // Calculate scope level from indentation\n    let scope_level = indent_level / base_indent;\n    \n    // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n    if let Some(existing_scope) = get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n        // Re-enter existing scope (e.g., sibling mapping)\n        // Don't clear keys - they persist within the scope\n        set_current_scope(existing_scope);\n    } else {\n        // Create new scope for this indentation level\n        let new_scope = Scope {\n            indent_level,\n            keys: HashSet::new(),\n            start_line: line,\n            parent_key,\n            is_flow_style: false,\n        };\n        push_scope(new_scope);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### 2. Exiting a Scope (Indentation Decrease)\n\n```yaml\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\ndatabase:           # ← EXIT scope for \"services.web\"\n  host: db.example.com\n```\n\n**Algorithm:**\n```rust\nfn on_indent_decrease(new_indent_level: usize) {\n    // Pop scopes until we find the target scope\n    while current_scope().indent_level > new_indent_level {\n        pop_scope();\n    }\n    \n    // Set current to the scope matching new_indent_level\n    set_current_scope(get_scope_at_level(new_indent_level));\n}\n```\n\n**Key Preservation**: When exiting a scope, we **preserve** the scope and its keys. If we re-enter that scope later (e.g., a sibling mapping), the keys are still available for duplicate checking.\n\n### Key Detection and Classification\n\n#### Key Context Types\n\n```rust\n/// Classification of how a key is used in the YAML document\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\nenum KeyContext {\n    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n    InlineScalar {\n        key: String,\n        value: String,\n    },\n    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n    ParentMapping {\n        key: String,\n    },\n    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n    ParentSequence {\n        key: String,\n    },\n    /// Key in flow mapping: \"{key: value, key2: value2}\"\n    FlowMapping {\n        key: String,\n    },\n    /// Key with flow sequence value: \"key: [val1, val2]\"\n    FlowSequenceValue {\n        key: String,\n    },\n    /// Key in explicit mapping: \"? key\\n: value\"\n    ExplicitMapping {\n        key: String,\n    },\n}\n```\n\n#### Key Extraction Algorithm\n\n```rust\nfn extract_key_context(line: &str, line_num: usize) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n    let trimmed = line.trim();\n    \n    // Skip if not a key-value line\n    if !trimmed.contains(':') {\n        return None;\n    }\n    \n    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':').unwrap();\n    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos+1..];\n    \n    // Skip if key is empty or contains special characters\n    let key = key_part.trim();\n    if key.is_empty() || key.contains(['?', '&', '*', '!', '[', ']', '{', '}']) {\n        return None;\n    }\n    \n    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n        // \"key:\\n  ...\" → Parent key with nested content\n        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n    } else if after_colon.contains('[') {\n        // \"key: [val1, val2]\" → Key with flow sequence\n        KeyContext::FlowSequenceValue { key: key.to_string() }\n    } else if after_colon.contains('{') {\n        // \"key: {nested: value}\" → Key with flow mapping\n        KeyContext::FlowMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n    } else {\n        // \"key: value\" → Inline scalar value\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n            key: key.to_string(),\n            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n        }\n    };\n    \n    Some(context)\n}\n```\n\n### Duplicate Detection Algorithm\n\n```rust\nfn detect_duplicate_keys(\n    scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack,\n    line: &str,\n    line_num: usize,\n) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n    let mut errors = Vec::new();\n    \n    // Skip flow-style contexts (handled separately)\n    if is_in_flow_context(line) {\n        return errors;\n    }\n    \n    let indent = get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n    \n    // Handle scope transitions\n    match indent.cmp(&current_indent_level()) {\n        Ordering::Greater => {\n            // Entering deeper scope\n            if let Some(KeyContext::ParentMapping { key }) = extract_key_context(line, line_num) {\n                scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num, Some(key));\n            }\n            return errors; // Parent keys don't create duplicate checks\n        }\n        Ordering::Less => {\n            // Exiting to parent scope\n            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n        }\n        Ordering::Equal => {\n            // Same scope - continue checking for duplicates\n        }\n    }\n    \n    // Extract and check key\n    if let Some(key_context) = extract_key_context(line, line_num) {\n        match key_context {\n            KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } => {\n                // Parent keys create new scope, handled above\n            }\n            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } |\n            KeyContext::FlowMapping { key, .. } |\n            KeyContext::FlowSequenceValue { key, .. } => {\n                // Check for duplicate in current scope\n                if scope_stack.contains_key(&key) {\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n                        format!(\"duplicate key '{}' in mapping scope\", key)\n                    ).with_line(line_num).with_code(ErrorCode::KEY_DUPLICATE));\n                } else {\n                    scope_stack.add_key(key, line_num);\n                }\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n    }\n    \n    errors\n}\n```\n\n## Handling Complex Scenarios\n\n### Scenario 1: Deeply Nested Mappings\n\n```yaml\nlevel1:\n  level2:\n    level3:\n      key1: value1\n      key2: value2\n    key3: value3\n  key4: value4\nkey5: value5\n```\n\n**Scope Stack Evolution:**\n\n1. Line 1 (`level1:`): Enter scope level 0 with parent_key=\"level1\"\n2. Line 2 (`  level2:`): Enter scope level 2 with parent_key=\"level2\"\n3. Line 3 (`    level3:`): Enter scope level 4 with parent_key=\"level3\"\n4. Line 4 (`      key1:`): Add \"key1\" to scope at level 4\n5. Line 5 (`      key2:`): Add \"key2\" to scope at level 4\n6. Line 6 (`    key3:`): Exit to scope level 2, add \"key3\"\n7. Line 7 (`  key4:`): Exit to scope level 0, add \"key4\"\n8. Line 8 (`key5:`): Exit to root scope, add \"key5\"\n\n**Result**: Each \"key\" is tracked in its appropriate scope, no false duplicates.\n\n### Scenario 2: Sibling Mappings with Same Keys\n\n```yaml\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n```\n\n**Scope Stack Evolution:**\n\n1. Line 1 (`services:`): Root scope (level 0)\n2. Line 2 (`  web:`): Enter scope level 2, parent_key=\"web\"\n3. Line 3 (`    host:`): Add \"host\" to scope level 4\n4. Line 4 (`    port:`): Add \"port\" to scope level 4\n5. Line 5 (`  database:`): Exit to scope level 2, **preserve scope level 4 keys**\n6. Line 6 (`    host:`): **Enter existing scope level 4**, check \"host\" against **fresh keys**\n7. Line 7 (`    port:`): Add \"port\" to scope level 4\n\n**Critical Decision**: When exiting scope level 4 (going to level 2), do we:\n- **Option A**: Clear the scope level 4 keys (current behavior)\n- **Option B**: Preserve the scope level 4 keys for potential re-entry\n\n**Correct Behavior is Option A**: Each sibling mapping is a **new scope**. The \"host\" in `services.web` and the \"host\" in `services.database` are in **different scopes**, so they should both be allowed.\n\n**Revised Algorithm**:\n```rust\nfn on_indent_decrease(new_indent_level: usize) {\n    // Clear all scopes deeper than new_indent_level\n    scopes.retain(|scope| scope.indent_level <= new_indent_level);\n    set_current_scope(new_indent_level);\n}\n\nfn on_indent_increase(indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n    // Always create a FRESH scope when entering a deeper level\n    let new_scope = Scope {\n        indent_level,\n        keys: HashSet::new(),  // Fresh keys\n        start_line: line,\n        parent_key,\n        is_flow_style: false,\n    };\n    push_scope(new_scope);\n}\n```\n\n### Scenario 3: Mixed Scalar and Mapping Values\n\n```yaml\nsimple_key: scalar_value\nmapping_key:\n  nested1: value1\n  nested2: value2\nsequence_key:\n  - item1\n  - item2\nflow_key: {inline: value, another: value2}\n```\n\n**Handling**:\n- `simple_key`: Add \"simple_key\" to root scope\n- `mapping_key`: Add \"mapping_key\" to root scope, **then** enter nested scope\n- `sequence_key`: Add \"sequence_key\" to root scope, enter sequence scope\n- `flow_key`: Add \"flow_key\" to root scope, parse flow mapping separately\n\n### Scenario 4: Flow-Style Mappings\n\n```yaml\ninline: {key1: value1, key2: value2}\nmultiline: {\n  key3: value3,\n  key4: value4\n}\n```\n\n**Handling**:\n- Detect flow context by checking for `{` character\n- Extract keys using specialized flow parser\n- Track duplicates within the flow mapping scope only\n- Don't mix flow-style keys with block-style keys\n\n## Migration Path from Current Implementation\n\n### Phase 1: Add Scope Stack Alongside Current Implementation\n\n**Changes to `StructureState`:**\n\n```rust\nstruct StructureState {\n    // NEW: Hierarchical scope tracking\n    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n    \n    // LEGACY: Keep for backward compatibility during migration\n    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,\n    current_keys: HashSet<String>,\n    prev_indent: usize,\n    \n    // Migration flag\n    use_legacy_tracking: bool,\n}\n```\n\n**Dual Implementation:**\n```rust\nfn detect_duplicate_key_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n    if self.use_legacy_tracking {\n        // Use current implementation\n        self.legacy_detect_duplicates(line, line_num, errors);\n    } else {\n        // Use new scope-based implementation\n        self.scope_based_detect_duplicates(line, line_num, errors);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Phase 2: Feature Flag for New Implementation\n\n**Configuration Update:**\n\n```rust\npub struct DetectorConfig {\n    // ... existing config ...\n    \n    /// Enable scope-based key tracking (Phase 2 feature)\n    pub use_scope_based_key_tracking: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for DetectorConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            // ... existing defaults ...\n            use_scope_based_key_tracking: false,  // Disabled by default during migration\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Phase 3: Validation and Testing\n\n**Test Coverage:**\n\n```rust\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod scope_based_tests {\n    #[test]\n    fn test_sibling_mappings_same_keys() {\n        let yaml = \"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n\";\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::with_config(DetectorConfig {\n            use_scope_based_key_tracking: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        });\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n        assert!(errors.is_empty(), \"Should not report duplicate across siblings\");\n    }\n    \n    #[test]\n    fn test_actual_duplicate_in_same_scope() {\n        let yaml = \"\nconfig:\n  host: localhost\n  host: duplicate\n\";\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::with_config(DetectorConfig {\n            use_scope_based_key_tracking: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        });\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.code == ErrorCode::KEY_DUPLICATE));\n    }\n    \n    #[test]\n    fn test_deeply_nested_mappings() {\n        let yaml = \"\nlevel1:\n  level2:\n    level3:\n      key: value\n  key: value2\nkey: value3\n\";\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::with_config(DetectorConfig {\n            use_scope_based_key_tracking: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        });\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n        assert!(errors.is_empty(), \"Should handle deep nesting correctly\");\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Phase 4: Gradual Rollout\n\n1. **Week 1-2**: Feature flag = false (current behavior)\n2. **Week 3-4**: Feature flag = true in test environments only\n3. **Week 5-6**: Monitor error reports, tune scope detection\n4. **Week 7-8**: Feature flag = true in production\n5. **Week 9+**: Remove legacy code, set feature flag to always true\n\n### Phase 5: Remove Legacy Implementation\n\n```rust\n// Final StructureState (post-migration)\nstruct StructureState {\n    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n    // No legacy fields\n}\n```\n\n## Data Structure Summary\n\n### Complete Scope Tracking Structure\n\n```rust\n/// Hierarchical key tracking with proper scope isolation\npub struct ScopeBasedKeyTracker {\n    /// Stack of active scopes\n    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n    /// Base indentation size\n    base_indent: usize,\n    /// Current flow-style context (if any)\n    flow_context: Option<FlowContext>,\n}\n\n/// A single scope in the hierarchy\npub struct Scope {\n    /// Indentation level for this scope\n    pub indent_level: usize,\n    /// Keys defined in this scope\n    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Line where this scope started\n    pub start_line: usize,\n    /// Parent key name\n    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Whether this is a flow-style mapping\n    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n    /// Keys seen in flow context (if is_flow_style)\n    pub flow_keys: HashSet<String>,\n}\n\n/// Flow-style context tracking\npub struct FlowContext {\n    /// Brackets/braces depth\n    depth: usize,\n    /// Keys seen in current flow mapping\n    keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Opening line number\n    start_line: usize,\n}\n```\n\n## Algorithm Complexity\n\n### Time Complexity\n\n- **Scope lookup**: O(1) using indent_level as index\n- **Key duplicate check**: O(1) using HashSet\n- **Scope transition**: O(k) where k = number of scopes to pop (typically small)\n- **Overall line processing**: O(1) amortized per line\n\n### Space Complexity\n\n- **Per scope**: O(m) where m = number of keys in that scope\n- **Total space**: O(n * m_avg) where n = number of active scopes\n- **Typical case**: O(depth * keys_per_scope) where depth ≤ 10 for most YAML files\n\n## Error Messages\n\n### Duplicate Key Error\n\n```\nLine 5: duplicate key 'host' in mapping scope 'services.web'\n  First occurrence: Line 3\n  Current occurrence: Line 5\n  Scope path: services.web\n```\n\n### Enhanced Error Context\n\n```rust\npub struct DuplicateKeyError {\n    pub key: String,\n    pub scope_path: String,        // e.g., \"services.web\"\n    pub first_line: usize,\n    pub duplicate_line: usize,\n    pub code: ErrorCode,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\\n  Current location: Line {}\",\n            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line, self.duplicate_line\n        )\n    }\n}\n```\n\n## Configuration Options\n\n### DetectorConfig Extensions\n\n```rust\npub struct DetectorConfig {\n    // ... existing fields ...\n    \n    /// Enable scope-based key tracking\n    pub use_scope_based_key_tracking: bool,\n    \n    /// Base indentation size (2 or 4)\n    pub base_indent_size: usize,\n    \n    /// How strict duplicate detection should be\n    pub duplicate_key_strictness: DuplicateKeyStrictness,\n}\n\npub enum DuplicateKeyStrictness {\n    /// Only check within the same immediate scope\n    SameScopeOnly,\n    /// Check across entire scope hierarchy\n    FullHierarchy,\n    /// Allow duplicates if types differ\n    AllowDifferentTypes,\n}\n```\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThis design provides a robust, hierarchical scope-based key tracking system that:\n\n1. **Properly isolates keys by scope**: Each mapping maintains its own key set\n2. **Handles complex nesting**: Deep hierarchies are tracked correctly\n3. **Supports mixed content**: Scalar values, mappings, and sequences coexist\n4. **Provides clear migration path**: Feature flag and phased rollout\n5. **Maintains performance**: O(1) operations per line for typical cases\n6. **Enhances error messages**: Clear scope path in duplicate reports\n\nThe implementation will be rolled out in phases to ensure backward compatibility and thorough testing before making it the default behavior.\n\n## Next Steps\n\n1. Create migration plan with feature flags\n6. Monitor and validate in test environments\n7. Gradual rollout to production\n8. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_f742c3189c334456b51d84d9","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Scope-Based Key Tracking Demonstration\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This example demonstrates the hierarchical scope-based key tracking\n4\t//! system for YAML parsing, showing how it properly handles nested\n5\t//! mappings and avoids false duplicate positives.\n6\t\n7\tuse std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};\n8\tuse std::fmt;\n9\t\n10\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n11\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n12\tstruct Scope {\n13\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n14\t    indent_level: usize,\n15\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n16\t    keys: HashSet<String>,\n17\t    /// Line number where this scope started\n18\t    start_line: usize,\n19\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n20\t    parent_key: Option<String>,\n21\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n22\t    is_flow_style: bool,\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\timpl Scope {\n26\t    /// Create a new scope\n27\t    fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n28\t        Self {\n29\t            indent_level,\n30\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n31\t            start_line,\n32\t            parent_key,\n33\t            is_flow_style: false,\n34\t        }\n35\t    }\n36\t\n37\t    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n38\t    fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n39\t        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n40\t    }\n41\t\n42\t    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n43\t    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n44\t        self.keys.contains(key)\n45\t    }\n46\t}\n47\t\n48\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n49\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n50\tstruct ScopeStack {\n51\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n52\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n53\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n54\t    base_indent: usize,\n55\t}\n56\t\n57\timpl ScopeStack {\n58\t    /// Create a new scope stack\n59\t    fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n60\t        Self {\n61\t            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n62\t            base_indent,\n63\t        }\n64\t    }\n65\t\n66\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n67\t    fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n68\t        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n69\t    }\n70\t\n71\t    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n72\t    fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n73\t        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n74\t    }\n75\t\n76\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n77\t    fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n78\t        // Calculate scope level from indentation\n79\t        let scope_level = indent_level / self.base_indent;\n80\t\n81\t        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n82\t        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n83\t            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n84\t            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n85\t            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n86\t            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n87\t\n88\t            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n89\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n90\t            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n91\t        } else {\n92\t            // Create new scope\n93\t            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n94\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n95\t        }\n96\t    }\n97\t\n98\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n99\t    fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n100\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n101\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n102\t\n103\t        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n104\t        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n105\t            // This shouldn't happen in valid YAML, but handle gracefully\n106\t            let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n107\t            self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n108\t        }\n109\t    }\n110\t\n111\t    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n112\t    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n113\t        self.scopes.last()\n114\t            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n115\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n119\t    fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n120\t        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n121\t\n122\t        // Check if key exists before adding\n123\t        if self.contains_key(key) {\n124\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n125\t            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n126\t                key: key.to_string(),\n127\t                scope_path,\n128\t                first_line: scope.start_line,\n129\t                duplicate_line: line,\n130\t            })\n131\t        } else {\n132\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n133\t            scope.add_key(key);\n134\t            Ok(())\n135\t        }\n136\t    }\n137\t\n138\t    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n139\t    fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n140\t        let mut path = Vec::new();\n141\t        for scope in &self.scopes {\n142\t            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n143\t                path.push(key.clone());\n144\t            }\n145\t        }\n146\t        path.join(\".\")\n147\t    }\n148\t\n149\t    /// Get current indent level\n150\t    fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n151\t        self.scopes.last()\n152\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n153\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n154\t    }\n155\t}\n156\t\n157\t/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n158\t#[derive(Debug)]\n159\tstruct DuplicateKeyError Scope-Based Key Tracking Demonstration\n//!\n//! This example demonstrates the hierarchical scope-based key tracking\n//! system for YAML parsing, showing how it properly handles nested\n//! mappings and avoids false duplicate positives.\n\nuse std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct Scope {\n    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n    indent_level: usize,\n    /// Keys defined within this scope\n    keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Line number where this scope started\n    start_line: usize,\n    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n    parent_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n    is_flow_style: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Scope {\n    /// Create a new scope\n    fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            indent_level,\n            keys: HashSet::new(),\n            start_line,\n            parent_key,\n            is_flow_style: false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n    fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.keys.contains(key)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct ScopeStack {\n    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n    base_indent: usize,\n}\n\nimpl ScopeStack {\n    /// Create a new scope stack\n    fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n            base_indent,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n    fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n    fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n    }\n\n    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n    fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n        // Calculate scope level from indentation\n        let scope_level = indent_level / self.base_indent;\n\n        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n\n            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n        } else {\n            // Create new scope\n            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n    fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n\n        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n            // This shouldn't happen in valid YAML, but handle gracefully\n            let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n            self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to current scope\n    fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n\n        // Check if key exists before adding\n        if self.contains_key(key) {\n            let scope = self.current_scope();\n            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n                key: key.to_string(),\n                scope_path,\n                first_line: scope.start_line,\n                duplicate_line: line,\n            })\n        } else {\n            let scope = self.current_scope();\n            scope.add_key(key);\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n    fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n        let mut path = Vec::new();\n        for scope in &self.scopes {\n            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n                path.push(key.clone());\n            }\n        }\n        path.join(\".\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get current indent level\n    fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct DuplicateKeyError {\n    key: String,\n    scope_path: String,\n    first_line: usize,\n    duplicate_line: usize,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n        )\n    }\n}\n\n/// Key context classification\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\nenum KeyContext {\n    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n    InlineScalar { key: String, value: String },\n    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n    ParentMapping { key: String },\n    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n    ParentSequence { key: String },\n}\n\n/// Extract key context from a line\nfn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n    let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n    // Find colon position\n    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n\n    // Skip if key is empty\n    let key = key_part.trim();\n    if key.is_empty() {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n    } else {\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n            key: key.to_string(),\n            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n        }\n    };\n\n    Some(context)\n}\n\n/// Get leading whitespace length\nfn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\n    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n}\n\n/// Process YAML lines and detect duplicate keys\nfn process_yaml(yaml: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>, DuplicateKeyError> {\n    let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n    let mut processed_keys = Vec::new();\n\n    for (line_num, line) in yaml.lines().enumerate() {\n        let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n        // Skip empty lines and comments\n        if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let indent = get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n\n        // Handle scope transitions\n        use std::cmp::Ordering;\n        match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n            Ordering::Greater => {\n                // Entering deeper scope\n                if let Some(KeyContext::ParentMapping { ref key }) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Add the parent key to current scope first\n                    if let Err(e) = scope_stack.add_key(key, line_num_1index) {\n                        return Err(e);\n                    }\n                    // Then enter the deeper scope\n                    scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num_1index, Some(key.clone()));\n                    processed_keys.push(format!(\"Line {}: Enter scope '{}'\", line_num_1index, key));\n                    continue; // Parent keys already processed above\n                } else {\n                    // Not a parent mapping, just indent increase - enter anonymous scope\n                    scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num_1index, None);\n                    processed_keys.push(format!(\"Line {}: Enter scope at indent {}\", line_num_1index, indent));\n                }\n            }\n            Ordering::Less => {\n                // Exiting to parent scope\n                scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                processed_keys.push(format!(\"Line {}: Exit to scope at indent {}\", line_num_1index, indent));\n            }\n            Ordering::Equal => {\n                // Same scope - continue checking\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Extract and check key\n        if let Some(key_context) = extract_key_context(line) {\n            match key_context {\n                KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } => {\n                    // Handled in scope transition above\n                }\n                KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => {\n                    // Check for duplicate\n                    scope_stack.add_key(&key, line_num_1index)?;\n                    processed_keys.push(format!(\"Line {}: Added key '{}'\", line_num_1index, key));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(processed_keys)\n}\n\nfn main() {\n    println!(\"=== Scope-Based Key Tracking Demonstration ===\\n\");\n\n    // Example 1: Sibling mappings with same keys (should be OK)\n    println!(\"--- Example 1: Sibling Mappings ---\");\n    let yaml1 = \"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n\";\n\n    match process_yaml(yaml1) {\n        Ok(actions) => {\n            println!(\"✓ No duplicate key errors (expected)\");\n            for action in actions {\n                println!(\"  {}\", action);\n            }\n        }\n        Err(e) => println!(\"✗ Unexpected error: {}\", e),\n    }\n\n    println!();\n\n    // Example 2: Actual duplicate in same scope (should fail)\n    println!(\"--- Example 2: Actual Duplicate ---\");\n    let yaml2 = \"\nconfig:\n  host: localhost\n  host: duplicate\n\";\n\n    match process_yaml(yaml2) {\n        Ok(_) => println!(\"✗ Should have detected duplicate key\"),\n        Err(e) => println!(\"✓ Correctly detected duplicate:\\n  {}\", e),\n    }\n\n    println!();\n\n    // Example 3: Deeply nested mappings\n    println!(\"--- Example 3: Deep Nesting ---\");\n    let yaml3 = \"\nlevel1:\n  level2:\n    level3:\n      key: value1\n    key: value2\n  key: value3\nkey: value4\n\";\n\n    match process_yaml(yaml3) {\n        Ok(actions) => {\n            println!(\"✓ No duplicate key errors (expected)\");\n            for action in actions {\n                println!(\"  {}\", action);\n            }\n        }\n        Err(e) => println!(\"✗ Unexpected error: {}\", e),\n    }\n\n    println!();\n\n    // Example 4: Mixed scalar and mapping values\n    println!(\"--- Example 4: Mixed checks and provides\n183\t/// a unified interface for error detection.\n184\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n185\tpub struct SyntaxDetector {\n186\t    /// Configuration for detection behavior\n187\t
