/// Whether to validate delimiter balancing\n206\t    pub validate_delimiter_balance: bool,\n207\t    /// Whether to detect duplicate keys\n208\t    pub detect_duplicate_keys: bool,\n209\t    /// Whether to detect invalid sequence syntax\n210\t    pub detect_invalid_sequences: bool,\n211\t    /// Whether to detect invalid mapping syntax\n212\t    pub detect_invalid_mappings: bool,\n213\t}\n214\t\n215\timpl Default for DetectorConfig {\n216\t    fn default() -> Self {\n217\t        Self {\n218\t            detect_mixed_indentation: true,\n219\t            check_consistent_indentation: true,\n220\t            base_indent_size: 2,\n221\t            validate_delimiter_balance: true,\n222\t            detect_duplicate_keys: true,\n223\t            detect_invalid_sequences: true,\n224\t            detect_invalid_mappings: true,\n225\t        }\n226\t    }\n227\t}\n228\t\n229\t/// State tracking for indentation analysis\n230\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n231\tstruct IndentationState {\n232\t    /// Whether tabs have been seen in the file\n233\t    has_tabs: bool,\n234\t    /// Whether spaces have been seen in the file\n235\t    has_spaces: bool,\n236\t    /// Previous line's indentation level (in spaces)\n237\t    prev_indent_level: usize,\n238\t    /// Expected indentation levels for nested contexts\n239\t    indent_stack: Vec<usize>,\n240\t    /// Lines with mixed indentation\n241\t    mixed_lines: Vec<usize>,\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\t/// State tracking for delimiter analysis\n245\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n246\tstruct DelimiterState {\n247\t    /// Stack of opening brackets/braces\n248\t    bracket_stack: Vec<(char, usize)>, // (bracket_type, line_number)\n249\t    /// Current quote state (None, Single, Double)\n250\t    quote_state: Option<QuoteType>,\n251\t    /// Lines with quote errors\n252\t    quote_errors: Vec<usize>,\n253\t    /// Whether we're inside a multiline block (|, >)\n254\t    in_multiline_block: bool,\n255\t    /// Indentation level of the multiline block start\n256\t    multiline_block_indent: usize,\n257\t    /// Whether we're inside flow-style context (within [] or {})\n258\t    in_flow_context: bool,\n259\t}\n260\t\n261\t/// Quote type for tracking\n262\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n263\tenum QuoteType {\n264\t    Single,\n265\t    Double,\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t/// State tracking for structure analysis\n269\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n270\tstruct StructureState {\n271\t    /// Stack of nested structures (mapping, sequence, etc.)\n272\t    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,\n273\t    /// Keys seen at current indentation level\n274\t    current_keys: HashSet<String>,\n275\t    /// Previous line's indentation level\n276\t    prev_indent: usize,\n277\t}\n278\t\n279\t/// Current structure context\n280\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n281\tenum StructureContext {\n282\t    Mapping,\n283\t    Sequence,\n284\t    FlowMapping,\n285\t    FlowSequence,\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\timpl SyntaxDetector {\n289\t    /// Create a new syntax detector with default configuration\n290\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n291\t        Self::with_config(DetectorConfig::default())\n292\t    }\n293\t\n294\t    /// Create a new syntax detector with custom configuration\n295\t    pub fn with_config(config: DetectorConfig) -> Self {\n296\t        Self {\n297\t            config,\n298\t            indentation_state: IndentationState::default(),\n299\t            delimiter_state: DelimiterState::default(),\n300\t            structure_state: StructureState::default(),\n301\t        }\n302\t    }\n303\t\n304\t    /// Detect all syntax errors in YAML content\n305\t    ///\n306\t    /// This is the main entry point for syntax detection. It runs all\n307\t    /// enabled detectors and returns a comprehensive list of errors.\n308\t    ///\n309\t    /// # Arguments\n310\t    /// * `content` - The YAML content to analyze\n311\t    ///\n312\t    /// # Returns\n313\t    /// A vector of validation errors representing all detected syntax issues\n314\t    pub fn detect_errors(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n315\t        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n316\t        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n317\t\n318\t        // Reset state for new detection\n319\t        self.reset_state();\n320\t\n321\t        for (line_num, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n322\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1; // Convert to 1-indexed\n323\t\n324\t            // Skip empty lines and comments for most checks\n325\t            let is_meaningful = !line.trim().is_empty() && !line.trim().starts_with('#');\n326\t\n327\t            if is_meaningful {\n328\t                // Run all enabled detectors\n329\t                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation || self.config.check_consistent_indentation {\n330\t                    self.detect_indentation_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n331\t                }\n332\t\n333\t                if self.config.validate_delimiter_balance {\n334\t                    self.detect_delimiter_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n335\t                }\n336\t\n337\t                if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences || self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n338\t                    self.detect_structure_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n339\t                }\n340\t\n341\t                if self.config.detect_duplicate_keys {\n342\t                    self.detect_duplicate_key_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n343\t                }\n344\t            }\n345\t        }\n346\t\n347\t        // Final validation checks\n348\t        self.finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut errors);\n349\t        self.finalize_structure_checks(&mut errors);\n350\t\n351\t        errors\n352\t    }\n353\t\n354\t    /// Detect syntax errors and convert to ValidationResult\n355\t    pub fn detect_to_validation_result(&mut self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n356\t        let errors = self.detect_errors(content);\n357\t\n358\t        if errors.is_empty() {\n359\t            ValidationResult::success()\n360\t        } else {\n361\t            ValidationResult::failure(errors)\n362\t        }\n363\t    }\n364\t\n365\t    /// Reset all state tracking for a new detection run\n366\t    fn reset_state(&mut self) {\n367\t        self.indentation_state = IndentationState::default();\n368\t        self.delimiter_state = DelimiterState::default();\n369\t        self.structure_state = StructureState::default();\n370\t    }\n371\t\n372\t    /// Detect indentation-related errors\n373\t    fn detect_indentation_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n374\t        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n375\t\n376\t        // Check for mixed tabs and spaces in leading whitespace\n377\t        if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n378\t            let leading_chars: Vec<char> = line.chars().take(leading_whitespace_len).collect();\n379\t            let has_tabs = leading_chars.contains(&'\\t');\n380\t            let has_spaces = leading_chars.contains(&' ');\n381\t\n382\t            if has_tabs && has_spaces {\n383\t                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n384\t                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n385\t                self.indentation_state.mixed_lines.push(line_num);\n386\t\n387\t                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::MixedTabsAndSpaces;\n388\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n389\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n390\t                    error_type.description()\n391\t                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n392\t            } else if has_tabs {\n393\t                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n394\t                // Optionally report tab-only indentation\n395\t                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation {\n396\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::TabCharacter;\n397\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n398\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n399\t                        format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n400\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n401\t                }\n402\t            } else if has_spaces {\n403\t                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n404\t            }\n405\t        }\n406\t\n407\t        // Check for consistent indentation levels\n408\t        if self.config.check_consistent_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n409\t            let indent_level = leading_whitespace_len;\n410\t\n411\t            // Check if indentation is a multiple of base_indent_size\n412\t            if indent_level % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n413\t                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentLevel;\n414\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n415\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n416\t                    format!(\"{}: {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n417\t                            error_type.code(), indent_level, self.config.base_indent_size)\n418\t                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n419\t            }\n420\t\n421\t            // Check if indentation increase is consistent\n422\t            if indent_level > self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n423\t                let increase = indent_level - self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level;\n424\t\n425\t                // Check if increase is too large (more than 2 levels at once)\n426\t                if increase > self.config.base_indent_size * 2 {\n427\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::ExcessiveIndentIncrease;\n428\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n429\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n430\t                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces exceeds maximum of {} levels\",\n431\t                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size * 2)\n432\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n433\t                }\n434\t\n435\t                if increase % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n436\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentIncrease;\n437\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n438\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n439\t                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n440\t                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size)\n441\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n442\t                } else {\n443\t                    // Valid increase - push to stack\n444\t                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.push(indent_level);\n445\t                }\n446\t            } else if indent_level < self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n447\t                // Decrease in indentation - pop from stack\n448\t                while !self.indentation_state.indent_stack.is_empty()\n449\t                      && *self.indentation_state.indent_stack.last().unwrap() > indent_level {\n450\t                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.pop();\n451\t                }\n452\t            }\n453\t\n454\t            self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level = indent_level;\n455\t        }\n456\t    }\n457\t\n458\t    /// Detect delimiter-related errors\n459\t    fn detect_delimiter_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n460\t        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n461\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n462\t\n463\t        // Check if we're inside a multiline block\n464\t        if self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block {\n465\t            // Multiline blocks continue until we find a line with less or equal indentation\n466\t            // that's not empty or a comment\n467\t            if leading_whitespace_len > self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent ||\n468\t               (leading_whitespace_len == self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent && (trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#'))) {\n469\t                return; // Skip lines inside the multiline block\n470\t            } else {\n471\t                // Exit multiline block when indentation decreases\n472\t                self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = false;\n473\t                self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = 0;\n474\t            }\n475\t        }\n476\t\n477\t        // Skip empty lines and comments (but not when inside multiline blocks - handled above)\n478\t        if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n479\t            return;\n480\t        }\n481\t\n482\t        // Handle document markers (---, ...)\n483\t        if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n484\t            return; // Document markers should not be flagged for missing colons\n485\t        }\n486\t\n487\t        // Handle multiline block scalars (|, >, |-, |- , >-, >+, |+)\n488\t        // These appear after a colon, like \"key: |\" or \"key: >\"\n489\t        if trimmed.contains(\": |\") || trimmed.contains(\": >\") ||\n490\t           trimmed.contains(\":|-\") || trimmed.contains(\":>-\") ||\n491\t           trimmed.contains(\":|+\") || trimmed.contains(\":>+\") {\n492\t            self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = true;\n493\t            self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = leading_whitespace_len; // Content must be MORE indented than this line\n494\t        }\n495\t\n496\t        // Check for missing colons after keys\n497\t        if !trimmed.starts_with('-') && // Not a sequence item\n498\t           !trimmed.starts_with('?') && // Not an explicit key\n499\t           !trimmed.starts_with(':') && // Not a value\n500\t           !trimmed.starts_with('&') && // Not an anchor\n501\t           !trimmed.starts_with('*') && // Not an alias\n502\t           !trimmed.starts_with('!') && // Not a tag\n503\t           !trimmed.starts_with('|') && // Not a literal block scalar\n504\t           !trimmed.starts_with('>') && // Not a folded block scalar\n505\t           !trimmed.contains('{') && // Not flow style mapping\n506\t           !trimmed.contains('[') && // Not flow style sequence\n507\t           !trimmed.contains(':') && // No colon at all\n508\t           !trimmed.is_empty() &&\n509\t           self.looks_like_key(trimmed) {\n510\t\n511\t            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MissingColon;\n512\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n513\t                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n514\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n515\t            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n516\t        }\n517\t\n518\t        // Track bracket/brace balance and flow context\n519\t        for (char_pos, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n520\t            match ch {\n521\t                '[' => {\n522\t                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('[', line_num));\n523\t                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n524\t                }\n525\t                ']' => {\n526\t                    if let Some(('[', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n527\t                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n528\t                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n529\t                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n530\t                    } else {\n531\t                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBracket;\n532\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n533\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n534\t                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n535\t                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n536\t                    }\n537\t                }\n538\t                '{' => {\n539\t                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('{', line_num));\n540\t                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n541\t                }\n542\t                '}' => {\n543\t                    if let Some(('{', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n544\t                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n545\t                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n546\t                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n547\t                    } else {\n548\t                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBrace;\n549\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n550\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n551\t                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n552\t                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n553\t                    }\n554\t                }\n555\t                '\\'' => {\n556\t                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n557\t                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Single),\n558\t                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n559\t                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => {\n560\t                            // Mismatched quote\n561\t                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n562\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n563\t                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n564\t                                format!(\"{}: {} (single inside double)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n565\t                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n566\t                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n567\t                        }\n568\t                    }\n569\t                }\n570\t                '\"' => {\n571\t                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n572\t                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Double),\n573\t                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n574\t                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => {\n575\t                            // Mismatched quote\n576\t                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n577\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n578\t                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n579\t                                format!(\"{}: {} (double inside single)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n580\t                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n581\t                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n582\t                        }\n583\t                    }\n584\t                }\n585\t                _ => {}\n586\t            }\n587\t        }\n588\t    }\n589\t\n590\t    /// Detect structure-related errors\n591\t    fn detect_structure_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n592\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n593\t\n594\t        // Detect invalid sequence syntax\n595\t        if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences {\n596\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n597\t                // Valid sequence item\n598\t                if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n599\t                   !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Sequence)) {\n600\t                    self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Sequence);\n601\t                }\n602\t            } else if trimmed.starts_with('-') && trimmed.len() > 1 {\n603\t                // Dash followed by something other than space\n604\t                if let Some(next_char) = trimmed.chars().nth(1) {\n605\t                    if !next_char.is_whitespace() {\n606\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n607\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n608\t                            \"sequence dash '-' must be followed by whitespace\"\n609\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n610\t                    }\n611\t                }\n612\t            }\n613\t        }\n614\t\n615\t        // Detect invalid mapping syntax\n616\t        if self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n617\t            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n618\t                let before_colon = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n619\t\n620\t                // Check if colon is properly used after a key\n621\t                if !before_colon.is_empty() && !before_colon.ends_with(' ') {\n622\t                    // Valid key: value pair\n623\t                    if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n624\t                       !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Mapping)) {\n625\t                        self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Mapping);\n626\t                    }\n627\t                } else if before_colon.is_empty() {\n628\t                    // Colon at start - might be a value or error\n629\t                    if !trimmed.starts_with(\": \") && trimmed != \":\" {\n630\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n631\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n632\t                            \"colon ':' at start must be followed by space\"\n633\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n634\t                    }\n635\t                }\n636\t            }\n637\t        }\n638\t\n639\t        // Check for flow syntax indicators\n640\t        if trimmed.contains('{') || trimmed.contains('[') {\n641\t            self.structure_state.context_stack.push(if trimmed.contains('{') {\n642\t                StructureContext::FlowMapping\n643\t            } else {\n644\t                StructureContext::FlowSequence\n645\t            });\n646\t        }\n647\t    }\n648\t\n649\t    /// Detect duplicate key errors\n650\t    fn detect_duplicate_key_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n651\t        // Skip duplicate key detection when inside flow-style contexts ([] or {})\n652\t        // Flow-style YAML uses {key: value} syntax which should not be treated as duplicate keys\n653\t        if self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context {\n654\t            return;\n655\t        }\n656\t\n657\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n658\t        let indent = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n659\t\n660\t        // Check if we're moving to a new context (indentation decreased)\n661\t        // This indicates we've exited a nested structure\n662\t        if indent < self.structure_state.prev_indent {\n663\t            // Clear keys when we exit a context\n664\t            self.structure_state.current_keys.clear();\n665\t        }\n666\t\n667\t        // Update previous indentation for next comparison\n668\t        self.structure_state.prev_indent = indent;\n669\t\n670\t        // Extract key if this is a key-value pair\n671\t        if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n672\t            let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n673\t\n674\t            // Check if this is a parent key (ends with colon and no value on same line)\n675\t            // A parent key typically has nothing after the colon or just whitespace/comments\n676\t            let after_colon = trimmed[colon_pos + 1..].trim();\n677\t            let is_parent_key = after_colon.is_empty() ||\n678\t                                after_colon.starts_with('#');\n679\t\n680\t            if is_parent_key {\n681\t                // Clear current keys when we encounter a new parent key\n682\t                self.structure_state.current_keys.clear();\n683\t                return;\n684\t            }\n685\t\n686\t            // Skip if it's a value or not a simple key\n687\t            if key_part.is_empty() || key_part.contains('-') || key_part.contains('?') {\n688\t                return;\n689\t            }\n690\t\n691\t            let key = key_part.trim();\n692\t\n693\t            // Skip quoted keys and special keys\n694\t            if key.starts_with('\\'') || key.starts_with('\"') || key.contains('#') {\n695\t                return;\n696\t            }\n697\t\n698\t            // Check for duplicates at this indentation level\n699\t            if self.structure_state.current_keys.contains(key) {\n700\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n701\t                    format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n702\t                    format!(\"duplicate key '{}' at same indentation level\", key)\n703\t                ).with_line(line_num));\n704\t            } else {\n705\t                self.structure_state.current_keys.insert(key.to_string());\n706\t            }\n707\t        }\n708\t    }\n709\t\n710\t    /// Finalize delimiter checks after processing all lines\n711\t    fn finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut self, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n712\t        // Check for unclosed brackets\n713\t        for (bracket, line_num) in &self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack {\n714\t            let error_type = match *bracket {\n715\t                '[' => DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedBracket,\n716\t                '{' => DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedBrace,\n717\t                _ => continue, // Skip unknown bracket types\n718\t            };\n719\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n720\t                \"delimiter_balance\".to_string(),\n721\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n722\t            ).with_line(*line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n723\t        }\n724\t\n725\t        // Check for unclosed quotes\n726\t        if self.delimiter_state.quote_state.is_some() {\n727\t            let (error_type, quote_char) = match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n728\t                Some(QuoteType::Single) => (DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedSingleQuote, \"'\"),\n729\t                Some(QuoteType::Double) => (DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedDoubleQuote, \"\\\"\"),\n730\t                None => unreachable!(),\n731\t            };\n732\t\n733\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n734\t                \"quote_balance\".to_string(),\n735\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n736\t            ).with_line(0).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type)); // Line 0 since we don't track where it opened\n737\t        }\n738\t    }\n739\t\n740\t    /// Finalize structure checks after processing all lines\n741\t    fn finalize_structure_checks(&mut self, _errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n742\t        // No-op - same-level duplicate detection is handled in detect_duplicate_key_errors\n743\t    }\n744\t\n745\t    /// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n746\t    fn get_leading_whitespace_length(&self, line: &str) -> usize {\n747\t        line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n748\t    }\n749\t\n750\t    /// Check if a line looks like a key definition\n751\t    fn looks_like_key(&self, trimmed: &str) -> bool {\n752\t        // Keys are typically alphanumeric with underscores, dashes, or dots\n753\t        // They don't start with special YAML characters\n754\t        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n755\t            return false;\n756\t        }\n757\t\n758\t        let first_char = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();\n759\t\n760\t        // Exclude lines that start with YAML special characters\n761\t        if matches!(first_char, '-' | ':' | '?' | '|' | '>' | '#' | '[' | ']' | '{' | '}' | '\"' | '\\'' | '!' | '&' | '*' | '%' | '@' | '`') {\n762\t            return false;\n763\t        }\n764\t\n765\t        // Look for word characters at the start\n766\t        trimmed.chars().next().map_or(false, |c| c.is_alphanumeric() || c == '_' || c == '.')\n767\t    }\n768\t}\n769\t\n770\timpl Default for SyntaxDetector {\n771\t    fn default() -> Self {\n772\t        Self::new()\n773\t    }\n774\t}\n775\t\n776\t#[cfg(test)]\n777\tmod tests {\n778\t    use super::*;\n779\t\n780\t    #[test]\n781\t    fn test_detect_mixed_indentation() {\n782\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n783\t        let yaml = \"key: value\\n\\t  bad: value\";\n784\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n785\t\n786\t        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n787\t        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"mixed tabs and spaces\")));\n788\t    }\n789\t\n790\t    #[test]\n791\t    fn test_detect_missing_colon() {\n792\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n793\t        let yaml = \"key value\\n  key2: value2\";\n794\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n795\t\n796\t        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n797\t        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"missing colon\")));\n798\t    }\n799\t\n800\t    #[test]\n801\t    fn test_detect_unmatched_bracket() assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n807\t        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"unclosed\")));\n808\t    }\n809\t\n810\t    #[test]\n811\t    fn test_valid_yaml_no_errors() {\n812\t YAML Syntax Error Detection Module\n//!\n//! This module provides comprehensive syntax error detection for YAML files.\n//! It detects three main categories of errors:\n//!\n//! 1. **Indentation Errors**: Mixed spaces/tabs, inconsistent indentation levels\n//! 2. **Delimiter Errors**: Missing colons, unbalanced brackets/braces, quote errors\n//! 3. **Structure Errors**: Invalid mappings, malformed sequences, duplicate keys\n//!\n//! ## Architecture\n//!\n//! The detector follows a line-by-line analysis approach:\n//!\n//! - LineTracker: Tracks line numbers and content for error reporting\n//! - IndentationDetector: Analyzes indentation patterns\n//! - DelimiterDetector: Checks delimiter consistency\n//! - StructureDetector: Validates YAML structure rules\n//!\n//! ## Usage\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::syntax_detector::{SyntaxDetector, SyntaxError};\n//!\n//! let detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n//! let yaml_content = \"key: value\\n  nested: value\";\n//! let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml_content);\n//!\n//! for error in errors {\n//!     println!(\"Line {}: {}\", error.line, error.message);\n//! }\n//! ```\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::types::{ValidationError, ValidationResult};\nuse std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Classification of indentation error types\n///\n/// This enum provides structured categorization of different indentation\n/// error types that can occur in YAML files.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum IndentationErrorType {\n    /// Mixed tabs and spaces in the same line\n    MixedTabsAndSpaces,\n    /// Indentation not a multiple of the base indent size\n    InvalidIndentLevel,\n    /// Indentation increase is too large (> 2 levels at once)\n    ExcessiveIndentIncrease,\n    /// Indentation increase is not a multiple of base indent size\n    InvalidIndentIncrease,\n    /// Tab character detected (when tabs are not allowed)\n    TabCharacter,\n}\n\nimpl IndentationErrorType {\n    /// Get a human-readable description of this error type\n    pub fn description(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces => \"mixed tabs and spaces in indentation\",\n            Self::InvalidIndentLevel => \"indentation level is not a multiple of base indent size\",\n            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease => \"indentation increase is too large\",\n            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease => \"indentation increase is not a multiple of base indent size\",\n            Self::TabCharacter => \"tab character detected in indentation\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a short code for this error type\n    pub fn code(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces => \"E001\",\n            Self::InvalidIndentLevel => \"E002\",\n            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease => \"E003\",\n            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease => \"E004\",\n            Self::TabCharacter => \"E005\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get all indentation error types\n    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {\n        &[\n            Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces,\n            Self::InvalidIndentLevel,\n            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease,\n            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease,\n            Self::TabCharacter,\n        ]\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for IndentationErrorType {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", self.code(), self.description())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Classification of delimiter error types\n///\n/// This enum provides structured categorization of different delimiter\n/// error types that can occur in YAML files.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum DelimiterErrorType {\n    /// Missing colon after a key\n    MissingColon,\n    /// Unmatched opening bracket '['\n    UnmatchedOpeningBracket,\n    /// Unmatched closing bracket ']'\n    UnmatchedClosingBracket,\n    /// Unclosed bracket '['\n    UnclosedBracket,\n    /// Unmatched opening brace '{'\n    UnmatchedOpeningBrace,\n    /// Unmatched closing brace '}'\n    UnmatchedClosingBrace,\n    /// Unclosed brace '{'\n    UnclosedBrace,\n    /// Mismatched quotes (single inside double or vice versa)\n    MismatchedQuotes,\n    /// Unclosed single quote\n    UnclosedSingleQuote,\n    /// Unclosed double quote\n    UnclosedDoubleQuote,\n}\n\nimpl DelimiterErrorType {\n    /// Get a human-readable description of this error type\n    pub fn description(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::MissingColon => \"missing colon after key\",\n            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBracket => \"unmatched opening bracket '['\",\n            Self::UnmatchedClosingBracket => \"unmatched closing bracket ']'\",\n            Self::UnclosedBracket => \"unclosed bracket '['\",\n            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBrace => \"unmatched opening brace '{'\",\n            Self::UnmatchedClosingBrace => \"unmatched closing brace '}'\",\n            Self::UnclosedBrace => \"unclosed brace '{'\",\n            Self::MismatchedQuotes => \"mismatched quotes\",\n            Self::UnclosedSingleQuote => \"unclosed single quote\",\n            Self::UnclosedDoubleQuote => \"unclosed double quote\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a short code for this error type\n    pub fn code(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::MissingColon => \"D001\",\n            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBracket => \"D002\",\n            Self::UnmatchedClosingBracket => \"D003\",\n            Self::UnclosedBracket => \"D004\",\n            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBrace => \"D005\",\n            Self::UnmatchedClosingBrace => \"D006\",\n            Self::UnclosedBrace => \"D007\",\n            Self::MismatchedQuotes => \"D008\",\n            Self::UnclosedSingleQuote => \"D009\",\n            Self::UnclosedDoubleQuote => \"D010\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get all delimiter error types\n    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {\n        &[\n            Self::MissingColon,\n            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBracket,\n            Self::UnmatchedClosingBracket,\n            Self::UnclosedBracket,\n            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBrace,\n            Self::UnmatchedClosingBrace,\n            Self::UnclosedBrace,\n            Self::MismatchedQuotes,\n            Self::UnclosedSingleQuote,\n            Self::UnclosedDoubleQuote,\n        ]\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for DelimiterErrorType {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", self.code(), self.description())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Main syntax detector for YAML content\n///\n/// This struct orchestrates all syntax detection checks and provides\n/// a unified interface for error detection.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SyntaxDetector {\n    /// Configuration for detection behavior\n    config: DetectorConfig,\n    /// Track indentation state across lines\n    indentation_state: IndentationState,\n    /// Track delimiter state (brackets, braces, quotes)\n    delimiter_state: DelimiterState,\n    /// Track structure state (keys, sequences, mappings)\n    structure_state: StructureState,\n}\n\n/// Configuration for syntax detection behavior\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct DetectorConfig {\n    /// Whether to detect tab/space mixing\n    pub detect_mixed_indentation: bool,\n    /// Whether to check for consistent indentation (multiples of base)\n    pub check_consistent_indentation: bool,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4)\n    pub base_indent_size: usize,\n    /// Whether to validate delimiter balancing\n    pub validate_delimiter_balance: bool,\n    /// Whether to detect duplicate keys\n    pub detect_duplicate_keys: bool,\n    /// Whether to detect invalid sequence syntax\n    pub detect_invalid_sequences: bool,\n    /// Whether to detect invalid mapping syntax\n    pub detect_invalid_mappings: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for DetectorConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            detect_mixed_indentation: true,\n            check_consistent_indentation: true,\n            base_indent_size: 2,\n            validate_delimiter_balance: true,\n            detect_duplicate_keys: true,\n            detect_invalid_sequences: true,\n            detect_invalid_mappings: true,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// State tracking for indentation analysis\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct IndentationState {\n    /// Whether tabs have been seen in the file\n    has_tabs: bool,\n    /// Whether spaces have been seen in the file\n    has_spaces: bool,\n    /// Previous line's indentation level (in spaces)\n    prev_indent_level: usize,\n    /// Expected indentation levels for nested contexts\n    indent_stack: Vec<usize>,\n    /// Lines with mixed indentation\n    mixed_lines: Vec<usize>,\n}\n\n/// State tracking for delimiter analysis\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct DelimiterState {\n    /// Stack of opening brackets/braces\n    bracket_stack: Vec<(char, usize)>, // (bracket_type, line_number)\n    /// Current quote state (None, Single, Double)\n    quote_state: Option<QuoteType>,\n    /// Lines with quote errors\n    quote_errors: Vec<usize>,\n    /// Whether we're inside a multiline block (|, >)\n    in_multiline_block: bool,\n    /// Indentation level of the multiline block start\n    multiline_block_indent: usize,\n    /// Whether we're inside flow-style context (within [] or {})\n    in_flow_context: bool,\n}\n\n/// Quote type for tracking\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\nenum QuoteType {\n    Single,\n    Double,\n}\n\n/// State tracking for structure analysis\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct StructureState {\n    /// Stack of nested structures (mapping, sequence, etc.)\n    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,\n    /// Keys seen at current indentation level\n    current_keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Previous line's indentation level\n    prev_indent: usize,\n}\n\n/// Current structure context\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\nenum StructureContext {\n    Mapping,\n    Sequence,\n    FlowMapping,\n    FlowSequence,\n}\n\nimpl SyntaxDetector {\n    /// Create a new syntax detector with default configuration\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::with_config(DetectorConfig::default())\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new syntax detector with custom configuration\n    pub fn with_config(config: DetectorConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            indentation_state: IndentationState::default(),\n            delimiter_state: DelimiterState::default(),\n            structure_state: StructureState::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect all syntax errors in YAML content\n    ///\n    /// This is the main entry point for syntax detection. It runs all\n    /// enabled detectors and returns a comprehensive list of errors.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content to analyze\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A vector of validation errors representing all detected syntax issues\n    pub fn detect_errors(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n\n        // Reset state for new detection\n        self.reset_state();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1; // Convert to 1-indexed\n\n            // Skip empty lines and comments for most checks\n            let is_meaningful = !line.trim().is_empty() && !line.trim().starts_with('#');\n\n            if is_meaningful {\n                // Run all enabled detectors\n                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation || self.config.check_consistent_indentation {\n                    self.detect_indentation_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n                }\n\n                if self.config.validate_delimiter_balance {\n                    self.detect_delimiter_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n                }\n\n                if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences || self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n                    self.detect_structure_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n                }\n\n                if self.config.detect_duplicate_keys {\n                    self.detect_duplicate_key_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Final validation checks\n        self.finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut errors);\n        self.finalize_structure_checks(&mut errors);\n\n        errors\n    }\n\n    /// Detect syntax errors and convert to ValidationResult\n    pub fn detect_to_validation_result(&mut self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n        let errors = self.detect_errors(content);\n\n        if errors.is_empty() {\n            ValidationResult::success()\n        } else {\n            ValidationResult::failure(errors)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Reset all state tracking for a new detection run\n    fn reset_state(&mut self) {\n        self.indentation_state = IndentationState::default();\n        self.delimiter_state = DelimiterState::default();\n        self.structure_state = StructureState::default();\n    }\n\n    /// Detect indentation-related errors\n    fn detect_indentation_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n\n        // Check for mixed tabs and spaces in leading whitespace\n        if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n            let leading_chars: Vec<char> = line.chars().take(leading_whitespace_len).collect();\n            let has_tabs = leading_chars.contains(&'\\t');\n            let has_spaces = leading_chars.contains(&' ');\n\n            if has_tabs && has_spaces {\n                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n                self.indentation_state.mixed_lines.push(line_num);\n\n                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::MixedTabsAndSpaces;\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                    error_type.description()\n                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n            } else if has_tabs {\n                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n                // Optionally report tab-only indentation\n                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation {\n                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::TabCharacter;\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                        format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n                }\n            } else if has_spaces {\n                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for consistent indentation levels\n        if self.config.check_consistent_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n            let indent_level = leading_whitespace_len;\n\n            // Check if indentation is a multiple of base_indent_size\n            if indent_level % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentLevel;\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                    format!(\"{}: {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n                            error_type.code(), indent_level, self.config.base_indent_size)\n                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n            }\n\n            // Check if indentation increase is consistent\n            if indent_level > self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n                let increase = indent_level - self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level;\n\n                // Check if increase is too large (more than 2 levels at once)\n                if increase > self.config.base_indent_size * 2 {\n                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::ExcessiveIndentIncrease;\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces exceeds maximum of {} levels\",\n                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size * 2)\n                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n                }\n\n                if increase % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentIncrease;\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size)\n                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n                } else {\n                    // Valid increase - push to stack\n                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.push(indent_level);\n                }\n            } else if indent_level < self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n                // Decrease in indentation - pop from stack\n                while !self.indentation_state.indent_stack.is_empty()\n                      && *self.indentation_state.indent_stack.last().unwrap() > indent_level {\n                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.pop();\n                }\n            }\n\n            self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level = indent_level;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect delimiter-related errors\n    fn detect_delimiter_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n        // Check if we're inside a multiline block\n        if self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block {\n            // Multiline blocks continue until we find a line with less or equal indentation\n            // that's not empty or a comment\n            if leading_whitespace_len > self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent ||\n               (leading_whitespace_len == self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent && (trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#'))) {\n                return; // Skip lines inside the multiline block\n            } else {\n                // Exit multiline block when indentation decreases\n                self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = false;\n                self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = 0;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Skip empty lines and comments (but not when inside multiline blocks - handled above)\n        if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Handle document markers (---, ...)\n        if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n            return; // Document markers should not be flagged for missing colons\n        }\n\n        // Handle multiline block scalars (|, >, |-, |- , >-, >+, |+)\n        // These appear after a colon, like \"key: |\" or \"key: >\"\n        if trimmed.contains(\": |\") || trimmed.contains(\": >\") ||\n           trimmed.contains(\":|-\") || trimmed.contains(\":>-\") ||\n           trimmed.contains(\":|+\") || trimmed.contains(\":>+\") {\n            self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = true;\n            self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = leading_whitespace_len; // Content must be MORE indented than this line\n        }\n\n        // Check for missing colons after keys\n        if !trimmed.starts_with('-') && // Not a sequence item\n           !trimmed.starts_with('?') && // Not an explicit key\n           !trimmed.starts_with(':') && // Not a value\n           !trimmed.starts_with('&') && // Not an anchor\n           !trimmed.starts_with('*') && // Not an alias\n           !trimmed.starts_with('!') && // Not a tag\n           !trimmed.starts_with('|') && // Not a literal block scalar\n           !trimmed.starts_with('>') && // Not a folded block scalar\n           !trimmed.contains('{') && // Not flow style mapping\n           !trimmed.contains('[') && // Not flow style sequence\n           !trimmed.contains(':') && // No colon at all\n           !trimmed.is_empty() &&\n           self.looks_like_key(trimmed) {\n\n            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MissingColon;\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n        }\n\n        // Track bracket/brace balance and flow context\n        for (char_pos, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n            match ch {\n                '[' => {\n                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('[', line_num));\n                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n                }\n                ']' => {\n                    if let Some(('[', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n                    } else {\n                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBracket;\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n                    }\n                }\n                '{' => {\n                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('{', line_num));\n                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n                }\n                '}' => {\n                    if let Some(('{', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n                    } else {\n                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBrace;\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n                    }\n                }\n                '\\'' => {\n                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Single),\n                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => {\n                            // Mismatched quote\n                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                                format!(\"{}: {} (single inside double)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                '\"' => {\n                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Double),\n                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => {\n                            // Mismatched quote\n                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                                format!(\"{}: {} (double inside single)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect structure-related errors\n    fn detect_structure_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n        // Detect invalid sequence syntax\n        if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences {\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                // Valid sequence item\n                if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n                   !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Sequence)) {\n                    self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Sequence);\n                }\n            } else if trimmed.starts_with('-') && trimmed.len() > 1 {\n                // Dash followed by something other than space\n                if let Some(next_char) = trimmed.chars().nth(1) {\n                    if !next_char.is_whitespace() {\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                            \"sequence dash '-' must be followed by whitespace\"\n                        ).with_line(line_num));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Detect invalid mapping syntax\n        if self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n                let before_colon = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n\n                // Check if colon is properly used after a key\n                if !before_colon.is_empty() && !before_colon.ends_with(' ') {\n                    // Valid key: value pair\n                    if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n                       !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Mapping)) {\n                        self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Mapping);\n                    }\n                } else if before_colon.is_empty() {\n                    // Colon at start - might be a value or error\n                    if !trimmed.starts_with(\": \") && trimmed != \":\" {\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                            \"colon ':' at start must be followed by space\"\n                        ).with_line(line_num));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for flow syntax indicators\n        if trimmed.contains('{') || trimmed.contains('[') {\n            self.structure_state.context_stack.push(if trimmed.contains('{') {\n                StructureContext::FlowMapping\n            } else {\n                StructureContext::FlowSequence\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect duplicate key errors\n    fn detect_duplicate_key_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        // Skip duplicate key detection when inside flow-style contexts ([] or {})\n        // Flow-style YAML uses {key: value} syntax which should not be treated as duplicate keys\n        if self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n        let indent = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n\n        // Check if we're moving to a new context (indentation decreased)\n        // This indicates we've exited a nested structure\n        if indent < self.structure_state.prev_indent {\n            // Clear keys when we exit a context\n            self.structure_state.current_keys.clear();\n        }\n\n        // Update previous indentation for next comparison\n        self.structure_state.prev_indent = indent;\n\n        // Extract key if this is a key-value pair\n        if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n            let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n\n            // Check if this is a parent key (ends with colon and no value on same line)\n            // A parent key typically has nothing after the colon or just whitespace/comments\n            let after_colon = trimmed[colon_pos + 1..].trim();\n            let is_parent_key = after_colon.is_empty() ||\n                                after_colon.starts_with('#');\n\n            if is_parent_key {\n                // Clear current keys when we encounter a new parent key\n                self.structure_state.current_keys.clear();\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Skip if it's a value or not a simple key\n            if key_part.is_empty() || key_part.contains('-') || key_part.contains('?') {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            let key = key_part.trim();\n\n            // Skip quoted keys and special keys\n            if key.starts_with('\\'') || key.starts_with('\"') || key.contains('#') {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Check for duplicates at this indentation level\n            if self.structure_state.current_keys.contains(key) {\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n                    format!(\"duplicate key '{}' at same indentation level\", key)\n                ).with_line(line_num));\n            } else {\n                self.structure_state.current_keys.insert(key.to_string());\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Finalize delimiter checks after processing all lines\n    fn finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut self, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        // Check for unclosed brackets\n        for (bracket, line_num) in &self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack {\n            let error_type = match *bracket {\n                '[' => DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedBracket,\n                '{' => DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedBrace,\n                _ => continue, // Skip unknown bracket types\n            };\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                \"delimiter_balance\".to_string(),\n                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n            ).with_line(*line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n        }\n\n        // Check for unclosed quotes\n        if self.delimiter_state.quote_state.is_some() {\n            let (error_type, quote_char) = match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n                Some(QuoteType::Single) => (DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedSingleQuote, \"'\"),\n                Some(QuoteType::Double) => (DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedDoubleQuote, \"\\\"\"),\n                None => unreachable!(),\n            };\n\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                \"quote_balance\".to_string(),\n                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n            ).with_line(0).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type)); // Line 0 since we don't track where it opened\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Finalize structure checks after processing all lines\n    fn finalize_structure_checks(&mut self, _errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        // No-op - same-level duplicate detection is handled in detect_duplicate_key_errors\n    }\n\n    /// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n    fn get_leading_whitespace_length(&self, line: &str) -> usize {\n        line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a line looks like a key definition\n    fn looks_like_key(&self, trimmed: &str) -> bool {\n        // Keys are typically alphanumeric with underscores, dashes, or dots\n        // They don't start with special YAML characters\n        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let first_char = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();\n\n        // Exclude lines that start with YAML special characters\n        if matches!(first_char, '-' | ':' | '?' | '|' | '>' | '#' | '[' | ']' | '{' | '}' | '\"' | '\\'' | '!' | '&' | '*' | '%' | '@' | '`') {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Look for word characters at the start\n        trimmed.chars().next().map_or(false, |c| c.is_alphanumeric() || c == '_' || c == '.')\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for SyntaxDetector {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_mixed_indentation() {\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n        let yaml = \"key: value\\n\\t  bad: value\";\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n\n        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"mixed tabs and spaces\")));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_missing_colon() {\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n        let yaml = \"key value\\n  key2: value2\";\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n\n        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"missing colon\")));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_unmatched_bracket() {\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n        let yaml = \"key: [value1, value2\";\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n\n        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"unclosed\")));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_valid_yaml_no_errors() {\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n        let yaml = \"key: value\\n  nested:\\n    - item1\\n    - item2\";\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n\n        assert!(errors.is_empty());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":819,"startLine":1,"totalLines":819}}}
